articles.title: Richard Scolyer, cancer researcher and former Australian of the year, dies aged 59
articles.description: Scolyer, who did pioneering work on immunotherapy, was diagnosed with inoperable brain cancer in 2023 Prof Richard Scolyer, the world-renowned cancer researcher and former Australian of the year, has died at the age of 59. Scolyer’s family shared a statement the eminent pathologist and melanoma expert penned before his final stages of illness. Continue reading...

articles.title: There's plenty of life left in me! Sir Chris Hoy's message of hope
articles.description: Scots cycling legend Sir Chris Hoy wants to redefine the perception of people living with stage four cancer and insists: 'There's plenty of life left in us.'

articles.title: Alaska health officials advance over 400 projects for Rural Health Transformation funding
articles.description: The projects were selected from nearly 1,800 proposals that came from more than 800 organizations and companies.

articles.title: I lost 80 pounds on a GLP‐1—Hers is making treatment more accessible
articles.description: Hers launches a GLP‐1 weight loss membership starting at $39. Here’s how the program works, what’s included, and who may qualify.

articles.title: Ultra-processed foods linked to 58 per cent rise in dementia risk
articles.description: Processed meats contribute most to cognitive decline, a new Harvard study has found. But some foods have the opposite effect.

articles.title: Record number of people waiting for NHS diagnostic tests in England
articles.description: One in five of the 1.92m patients on list wait longer than six weeks for tests such as CT and MRI scans, analysis shows A record number of people are waiting for a diagnostic test on the NHS, triggering fears that delays in accessing CT and MRI scans could endanger patients’ health. A total of 1.92 million patients in England are waiting to have a test to diagnose their illness such as by an ultrasound scan, assessment of their hearing, bone scan or various tests for cancer. The diagnostic waiting list has grown by 500,000 since 2022. It is 83% higher than before the Covid pandemic. On current trends the waiting list will hit 2 million in March 2027. Continue reading...

articles.title: ‘Significant breakthrough’: NHS hospitals adopt faster, more accurate bladder cancer test
articles.description: Five hospitals in England and Wales have switched to test taken at home, rather than invasive hospital procedure NHS hospitals are using a new way of diagnosing bladder cancer that is faster, more accurate and more convenient for patients than the existing test. Doctors said the Galeas bladder test is a major breakthrough because it involves a urine test taken at home rather than an invasive procedure done at hospital which is uncomfortable for patients. Continue reading...

articles.title: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment
articles.description: A traditional Chinese medicinal root used for over a thousand years is attracting new scientific attention for its potential to combat hair loss. Studies suggest Polygonum multiflorum can block harmful hormones, activate hair-growth signals, protect follicles, and boost blood flow to the scalp. Researchers say the herb’s effects align remarkably well with both ancient descriptions and modern hair biology.

articles.title: The Scan Suggested Down Syndrome. The Doctor's Next Words Stunned Me
articles.description: I want other families to know that there can still be so much joy, love, and beauty. My daughter is not defined by Down syndrome.

But having had her first son by C-section too, she was not convinced and pushed for tests, never expecting to be told her new bundle of joy had been born with an incredibly rare...

articles.title: Sanjit Biswas, Samsara CEO: Fortt Knox Earnings
articles.description: Samsara Co-Founder & CEO Sanjit Biswas joins Jon Fortt for an earnings update after a beat-and-raise Q1 FY27. Revenue of $478.8M topped a $455M consensus, and FY27 guidance was raised across revenue, operating margin, and EPS. Biswas attributes the strength to surging demand in physical operations, including data centers, grid modernization and roadway work — and frames Samsara's AI strategy as embedding practical AI at the frontline: driver briefings, weather updates, real-time site context.

articles.title: Going to bed at this time can increase loneliness and anxiety, new study finds

articles.title: Sanjay Beri, Netskope CEO: Fortt Knox Earnings
articles.description: Netskope Co-Founder & CEO Sanjay Beri joins Jon Fortt after a 22% stock drop following Q1 FY27 earnings that beat the headline but spooked investors on free cash flow. Beri defends the trajectory: Netskope is one of the fastest-growing cybersecurity companies, gross margins have climbed from the 60s to 77%, and Q1's FCF burn reflects a billings transition from multi-year upfront to annual that will turn FCF positive this year. AgentSkope is the new AI-agent moat.

articles.title: Tentative agreement reached for striking long term care workers
articles.description: Halifax--(BUSINESS WIRE)--A tentative agreement has been reached between the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) Local 1082 and St. Vincents Nursing Home. CUPE 1082 is the Lead Table in bargaining, responsible for negotiating terms that impact other bargaining tables, including setting the economic pattern for CUPE members in the long term care sector in Nova Scotia. Details of the tentative agreement will not be shared until members have had the opportunity to review and ratify the terms

articles.title: The best new cycling and ebike trails in Melbourne and surrounds
articles.description: Bike trails around Victoria have received extensive upgrades, including more spots for ebikes. With many trails accessible by public transport, it’s a great time to go for a ride Ebike sales are surging in Australia – and retailers are struggling to keep up. “We’ve sold three times as many ebikes for the first months of this year than we did last year,” says Tony Morgan, manager of The Electric Bicycle Company in Melbourne. “This is absolutely in response to the fuel price spikes.” Continue reading...

articles.title: Could a common blood pressure drug worsen kidney disease in type 2 diabetes?
articles.description: Dihydropyridine calcium-channel-blockers (DCCBs), used to treat high blood pressure, could speed up the progression of kidney disease in people with type 2 diabetes, a new study claims.
